$comment: Phase 7 (TODO-08) Agent-Task Routing AR2: Kahn topological sort over Sprint-1 story dependencies. Proves the dependency graph is a DAG (no cycles) and emits execution_groups = parallel waves (each group's stories have all dependencies satisfied by prior groups and no intra-group edge). Edges derived from sprint_1_backlog.md section 4 foundation-first rationale + AGREED CONTRACTS data-path handoffs. agile-business-mathematics-expert auto-invoked for the topological proof. Source: sprint_1_backlog.md, sprint_agent_briefs.json, _common_context.md.

edges.reason: Auth tenant substrate needs the tenant_id column from the Postgres schema.
Upload persists a documents row; needs the documents table/migrations.
Router reads sections (L1/L2) from Postgres to scope retrieval.
DPDP access reads documents/sections + pii_flags columns from the schema.
Embed+upsert targets the Qdrant collection + payload schema.
Router-side scoped-retrieval filter relies on the section_id/tenant_id payload index.
Upload endpoint mounts on the FastAPI service skeleton (Docker + health/ready).
Native TOC extraction runs on the uploaded/parsed document from the upload endpoint.
Section-aware chunking needs the extracted TOC section boundaries.
DPDP access endpoint is an authenticated, tenant-scoped op; needs auth/tenant substrate.

execution_groups_detail.note: Foundation layer: Postgres schema, Qdrant payload, service skeleton, CI gate. All in-degree 0; fully parallel. (DB engineer serializes 001 then 002; DevOps serializes 004 then 005 - cross-agent parallel, intra-agent sequential.)
Auth/tenant substrate, PDF upload endpoint, LangGraph router. All deps in wave 0. (Backend serializes 038 then 008; ai-engineer runs 014 in parallel.)
Native TOC extraction (after upload), DPDP access (after auth). Parallel across data-engineer and backend.
Embedding + idempotent upsert; needs chunks (011) + Qdrant payload (002).

proof.method: Kahn's algorithm: repeatedly emit all nodes with in-degree 0 as a parallel wave, remove their out-edges, decrement successors' in-degree, repeat.
proof.assertion: All 11 nodes were emitted across 5 waves with 0 nodes remaining; therefore no cycle exists and the graph is a proven DAG.
proof.critical_path_note: The ingestion chain (skeleton -> upload -> TOC -> chunk -> embed) is the critical path; it gates Sprint-1 end-to-end demo of upload -> route.
